message:
chore(.gitignore): untrack Cargo.lock — local dev patch pollutes it
in a way that would break `cargo install` for end users if committed

`scripts/dev-local-frankensqlite.toml` is a template that local
contributors copy into `.cargo/config.toml` to point every `fsqlite*`
crate at a sibling `../frankensqlite/crates/` path via
`[patch.crates-io]`. The template itself is checked in under
`scripts/` and `.cargo/config.toml` is already gitignored (line 61,
`.cargo/`), so the *patch configuration* is safely separated from
the repo.

The side-effect we couldn't catch that way: `Cargo.lock`. Any
`cargo build` / `cargo check` / `cargo test` run on a workstation
that has the patch applied rewrites Cargo.lock to remove the
`source = "registry+…"` and `checksum = "…"` lines for every
patched crate (they're path deps now, so cargo omits source +
checksum). That diff is 60+ hunks of "drop source/checksum on every
fsqlite* entry", and committing it would break `cargo install
beads_rust` for any user who doesn't have frankensqlite checked
out at the sibling path — their cargo would try to resolve the
same path deps and fail with "path dependency … not found".

Fix: add `Cargo.lock` to `.gitignore` and `git rm --cached` the
currently-tracked copy. Rationale documented inline in the ignore
rule so the next person who wonders "don't binary crates commit
Cargo.lock?" finds the answer without grepping history. Users
installing from crates.io are unaffected — `cargo publish`
regenerates the lockfile at publish time from the manifest. Users
installing via `cargo install --git` lose pin-exact reproducibility
of transitive versions, but gain a working install that doesn't
require the author's workstation layout.

The repo's Cargo.toml stays authoritative; removing the lockfile
does not change any resolved dependency version.

files.patch:
@@ -2,8 +2,15 @@
 target/
 target*/
 
-# Cargo lock for library crates (keep for binary crates)
 # Cargo.lock
+# Intentionally ignored: scripts/dev-local-frankensqlite.toml (copied to
+# .cargo/config.toml) patches fsqlite* deps at sibling paths, which rewrites
+# registry sources / checksums in Cargo.lock on every local `cargo` invocation.
+# Committing that dev-local lockfile would break `cargo install beads_rust`
+# for end users (paths point at /data/projects/frankensqlite on the author's
+# workstation). Keep Cargo.lock untracked — crates.io publishes regenerate
+# the lockfile from Cargo.toml on install.
+Cargo.lock
 
 # IDE
 .idea/
